content.text:
Research Methodology
Semester:
3  |
Type:
UET3  |
VHS:
21h  |
Weekly hours:
C: 1h30  |
Coefficient:
1  |
Credits:
2
Teaching Objectives
This module aims to teach students the skills and knowledge needed to conduct effective scientific research. It covers the key stages of research, from topic formulation to final report writing, including literature review, research design, data collection and analysis, and oral presentation of results.
Recommended prior knowledge:
Scientific writing, language used.
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction to Scientific Research
Definition and objectives of scientific research
Types of research (fundamental, applied, etc.)
Role of methodology in research
Importance of language mastery
Research ethics in computer science: plagiarism, self-plagiarism, generative AI (ChatGPT, Deepseek, etc.)
Research Topic Formulation
Identification and selection of a relevant research topic
Literature review: searching relevant sources (scientific articles, books, etc.); critical analysis of existing literature (state of the art); identifying gaps in the state of the art
Defining the research problem: formulating a clear research question; defining hypotheses
Research objectives: defining specific objectives; identifying expected results
Research Design
Choice of appropriate research methods (experimental, qualitative, quantitative, etc.)
Planning data collection (sampling, collection tools, etc.)
Developing a research plan
Data Collection
Implementing the research plan
Using data collection tools and techniques specific to computer science
Data management and organisation
Data Analysis
Use of data analysis software
Processing and analysis of collected data
Use of statistical methods or other appropriate analysis techniques
Interpretation of results and discussion of implications
Writing the Research Report
Publication modes and structures: article, patent, thesis, book, poster, oral communication
Use of writing software: LaTeX, Overleaf, etc.
Structure and organisation of a scientific research report
Clear and concise writing of different report sections
Bibliographic reference management tools (APA, IEEE, Zotero, EndNote, etc.)
Oral Presentation of Results
Preparation and delivery of an oral presentation of research results
Mastery of scientific communication
Assessment
In-person semester exam (100%)
References
Beaud, Michel (2020).
L'art de la thèse
. La Découverte.
Stefan Kottwitz (2021).
LaTeX Beginner's Guide
. 2nd Edition, Packt Publishing.
Jean-Marie Dubois (2005).
La rédaction scientifique: mémoires et thèses
. Estem.
Michèle Lenoble-Pinson (1996).
La rédaction scientifique: conception, rédaction, présentation
. De Boeck Université.
